title: Add investigator guardrails, templates, and skill allowlist
description: Create the investigator guardrail template and minimal role skill, then wire the skill allowlist and bundled template assets so the new agent type can run with read-only constraints plus a single declared report file.
acceptance_criteria: Root and bundled guardrail templates exist for `investigator`.
A minimal investigator role skill exists in project and bundled skill catalogs.
`AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST` contains an `investigator` entry limited to base orchestrator, investigator role, and memory.
Prompt/template asset tests cover the new built-in agent template availability.

handoff_summary.completed: All six deliverables implemented and committed: templates/agents/investigator.md (root guardrail), src/agent_takt/_data/templates/agents/investigator.md (bundled copy), .agents/skills/role/investigator/SKILL.md (role skill), src/agent_takt/_data/agents_skills/role/investigator/SKILL.md (bundled copy), AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST entry in skills.py (base-orchestrator + role/investigator + memory), and investigator added to test_templates_contain_all_agent_types in test_assets.py.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — this bead is fully implemented. test_prompts.py already exercises investigator via BUILT_IN_AGENT_TYPES iteration (no changes needed there).
handoff_summary.risks: None identified. The template starts with `# Investigator Guardrails` which satisfies the heading assertion in test_prompts.py. Both bundled assets resolve correctly via packaged_templates_dir() and packaged_agents_skills_dir().
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent should validate: (1) test_assets.py passes including the new investigator entry in test_templates_contain_all_agent_types, (2) test_prompts.py passes for the investigator subtest in test_load_guardrail_template and test_worker_prompt_references_every_builtin_template_file, (3) OrchestratorTests setUp copies investigator.md without error, (4) skills._skill_path resolves role/investigator from both project and bundled fallback.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. All new files are in previously unused paths. The only modifications to existing files (skills.py, test_assets.py) are additive.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Allowlist entry limited to base-orchestrator + role/investigator + memory (no capability or task skills) — investigator is read-only and doesn't need code-edit, test-execution, or docs-edit. Memory is included so the investigator can reference project context. 2. Template content is identical between root (templates/agents/) and bundled (_data/templates/agents/) copies — same pattern used by all other agent types. 3. Template heading is `# Investigator Guardrails` (not `# Investigator Agent Guardrails`) to match the standard non-recovery convention validated in test_prompts.py. 4. test_prompts.py required no changes — it already iterates BUILT_IN_AGENT_TYPES which includes investigator; the setUp in OrchestratorTests copies templates for all built-in types, so the new template file is all that was needed.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (a) test_assets.py::TestPackagedAssetsExistOnDisk::test_templates_contain_all_agent_types now checks investigator and passes; (b) test_prompts.py::PromptsTests::test_load_guardrail_template_returns_path_and_trimmed_contents_for_each_builtin_agent passes for investigator subtest (heading check + no trailing newline); (c) test_prompts.py::PromptsTests::test_worker_prompt_references_every_builtin_template_file passes for investigator; (d) skills.allowed_skill_ids('investigator') returns the expected three-entry tuple; (e) bundled fallback for role/investigator works when project skill dir is absent (mirrors existing TestSkillsFallbackToBundled pattern).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: N/A — implementation is complete as specified. The investigator output schema variant (findings/recommendations/risk_areas/report_path fields) was implemented in prior beads (B-e57b817b, B-30488ad5, B-a4b4dee5); this bead adds only the guardrail and skill layer.

conflict_risks: None. All new files are in previously unused paths. The only modifications to existing files (skills.py, test_assets.py) are additive.

execution_history.summary: Created investigator guardrail template, role skill, bundled copies of both, wired the AGENT_SKILL_ALLOWLIST entry, and extended the test_assets.py coverage for the new agent type.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
